thesis_status_reasoning: The system is under extreme asymmetric load with primary pumps at 98.5% capacity and flow at 19,500 GPM—just 500 GPM below the catastrophic leak threshold. The pump cavitation sensor shows WARNING_ELEVATED, indicating destructive conditions are developing. While gravity reserves remain at 98% and sector pressure is still adequate at 41.2 PSI, the system is on a knife's edge: if pumps fail to automatically trip offline during this cavitation warning, falsification criterion #5 is triggered. The thesis claims automated protection will sacrifice pumps to protect the whole, but this protection has not yet activated despite dangerous conditions.
action_reasoning: The automated SCADA logic should imminently trip the primary pumps offline to prevent cavitation damage, triggering gravity reserve deployment. Operators must watch for this automatic failover and verify that sector pressure remains above 20 PSI during the transition. Premature manual intervention could disrupt the automated protection sequence the thesis relies upon.

key_signals.signal: Pump cavitation sensor at WARNING_ELEVATED with 98.5% pump load
System flow at 19,500 GPM approaching 20,000 GPM catastrophic threshold
Gravity reserves at 98% with sector pressure at 41.2 PSI
Pumps have not automatically tripped despite sustained WARNING_ELEVATED cavitation state

biggest_uncertainty: Whether the automated pump protection will activate before cavitation progresses to CRITICAL_DAMAGE, as the thesis depends entirely on this fail-safe mechanism working as designed
